item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feels bad for him…

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
reading this made me sad for 36 hours.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necropost BUT yeah, Timmy here is Safe class as you can put the bird in a cage and leave it at that. Whatever changed him isn't important. We don't know how 99% of the anomalies are bad here, but that doesn't mean they are all Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah, my heartstrings
+1

item.title: Not really anomalous
item.content:encoded:
I mean, it's IMPLIED that the bird is supposed to be Timmy, but aside from knowing how to write… What proves that? There is more or less NO proof that this is anything other than a Parrot that knows how to scribble, and I don't see why the SCP Foundation would waste resources on it, especially when things in the Anomalous Items list have been destroyed for being useless.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh my GOD this was the first SCP that actually made me cry a bit

As someone who is a sucker for writing about sad things, this gets a +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 존나 소름끼쳐

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my headcanon for this now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very sad, +1. Perhaps the Foundation could assist Timmy in taking baths(videos?) or give him a keyboard he can tap out messages. Cartoons and educational games that don't depend on physical dexterity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adore birds, so this makes me sad on several different levels and the "why am I a bird?" really got to me.
my initial thought on what happened (oddly, it wasn't something I saw discussed here) is that this was the freak result of a head injury. though I suppose any explanation is upsetting.
I do agree with the weirdness of a kid learning to spell, but misspelling his own name since that's probably the first thing he learned to ever write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man this one always stick with me. I know essentially it's just a sapient animal but the whole thing where the kid got stuck into the bird, and especially since it implied it was done purposefully. Poor little Timmy. The sentence "wy am i a bird" makes me so incredibly sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait. Timmy. Timothy. Perhaps a connection?

item.title: Aw.
item.content:encoded: Upvoting because I actually felt pretty sorry for the bird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to raise him as my bird son.

Cockatoos are great at mimicry. Maybe this one was too good?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has got to be the saddest SCP I've seen on my time on the Wiki.
+1 for giving me tears man.

item.title: Just one issue...
item.content:encoded:
I hate to necro-post, but I have a little issue with this one that is really bugging me. Having him make spelling errors is believable, if he's supposed to be around 7, but the FIRST WORD that most people learn to write is their own name. So I find it really off that he writes "Timmi" and not Timmy.

Unless that was intentional… O.o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ive read a lot of SCPs but this for some reason depresses me the the most.

"wy am i a bird?" :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: manly tears man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually find this unpleasantly sad. :(

Can't believe I forgot to upvote this.

Totally not swooping in at the last second to steal the 200 comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea, but I find this mind of cute.

item.title: My reaction to this skip, everytime.
item.content:encoded: ;_;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had two thoughts while reading this, and I can't decide which is more disturbing.

The first thought is the obvious Replaced by a Skinwalker theory, which is scary in its own right.

My second theory was that the Timmy at home is still the normal Timmy, but somehow his mind was copied in to a bird. This one makes me sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This went from hilarious, with the idea of a belligerent bird, to unnerving with the thing being a not-quite doppleganger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor little bird/child :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what's this about a tale?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right in the feels!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice, with the right mix of mystery and sympathy. Also thows the foundation tryign to care for him without breakign the formal tone of the article.

Having the actual kid be (apparently) still fine is what really makes this intriguing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As sad as this is, there's also a little bit of heartwarming in there. Namely, that the Foundation is genuinely trying to, at the least, entertain him so maybe he can forget about his situation, if only for a little while.

item.title: great RP
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote comments sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a good way to write an article that makes people sad :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the very first SCP to actually make me feel sad. Congrats.

item.title: Names
item.content:encoded: Although typical protocol for sapient SCPs is that they be addressed by their SCP numbers, that pretty much assumes that they've got at least adult mentality and can understand the concept of one person getting multiple names. Under the circumstances — and given the issues of identity and despair central to the theme of this story — it seems to me that it would be a better idea to address this SCP as "Timmy" (and, as such, that this be included in the containment procedures).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this one, and I didn't think it was that great, so no vote for me. Then I read the comments. And the discussions in the comments made me go back and read it again, and that time I 'got' it. It's the first time I've ever changed my mind based on the comments. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Timmy, how did you get to Newark?"

WHATS NUARK

"The city where you found Officer Blackbox, Timmy. How did you get there?"

I DONT KNO I FLYD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor little kid. Creepy that there's a Timmy still living there.

How/why did it end up biting a cop in Newark, though? Did they not ask it how it go there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my new favorites. Heck, I even want to make fan art this is just so God Damn sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Old post is old, but what tale is this? I don't remember seeing it before.

Edit: Also, am I the only person bothered by the picture showing his cage outside?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very sad. And I felt really sad until I read "wy am i a bird". Then I cracked up. The rest is great, however, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor Timmy. I feel so sad for him.

I was expecting his family was dead or disappeared but then… wham! all of them are alive and having a normal life including Timmy. This makes it even more tragic, because the happy life Timmy longs to still exists but he couldn't ever come back to it; there isn't a place for him.

Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not read this skip for a while but the recent tale brought me back.
Upvote for sheer depression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed reading this and the tale that went with it. Upvote for both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1523, a Fifthist artifact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You people make horrible things.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find this very effective. It elicits an emotional response and I'm left engaged with the unanswered questions.

I have to admit I took issue with the family in Montana not being investigated further to be confusing and frustrating as well, but I began to realize how much it contributes to the dynamics I mentioned above. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But it doesn't say that the bird was doing anything even vaguely anomalous. Timmy "accosted" the agent and was "acting erractically".

"This is agent 8675309, I have a possible skip here."
"Report"
"Well, there's this cockatoo, see, and that's kinda rare in Jersey, and it keeps flapping it's wings at me and won't leave me alone."
"…………Goddamit Johnson!"
*hang-up slam*

Edit: I still like it, I just would like to see something more explicit about how Timmy made himself known.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. I don't care if it was done before, I am interested in how well it is written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumebly because it's his job to investigate things that are probably anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because if they're normal, no reason to waste time and resources on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have trouble getting past the near lack of interest in the human Timmy. A cursory investigation of the family and that's it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, that was pretty great. A little sad, but I like that kind of stuff.
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are are a lot of talking animals on the site. Go figure. Going back through the list you'll find there is no more of an excess of cliches in the 1000 block. We're approaching 2000 SCP objects, and - barring complete originality - what actually matters is the quality of the writing, taking a step back and asking yourself 'is this a good SCP on its own?'.

Upvoting alongside RhettSarlin reasons. The writing is great, and there is enough under the surface of an already intriguing, well-presented concept to keep you guessing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only problem: I don't understand what made the agent bring the bird in for testing in the first place. I see it was behaving weird and approached the officer…but…is that it? I don't think the natural reaction to that is "we have to bring this in for testing." Otherwise, I really liked the story! The very subtle impression that there is a boy trapped in a Cockatoo and an imposter is living his life is indeed very scary! For that reason, up-vote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably. It's probably just my problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, and I'm sure something did something similar before that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't Bleach do something like this- a parakeet with the soul of a boy called Yūichi Shibata trapped in it? I keep thinking of that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually the child isn't dead. He's still living a happy life. And the bird can't talk, it has to write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's marked Euclid because of the shape-shifter/fact this boy was imprinted by some outside force into the body of a bird.

Otherwise yes. It's technically just a bird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree, you keep Timmy in a cage he can't destroy, he can't bend reality or whatnot, and voilá.

Anyway, it's a nice SCP, but I see it just as the boy's mind imprinted on the bird. No vote.
